home.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
_comment_home_faq: 6 SEO-optimized FAQs for the home page — covers: free, privacy/no upload, no account, available tools, file size limit, mobile support

home.faq.items.q: Are all the tools on FileYuzu free to use?
home.faq.items.a: Yes, every tool on FileYuzu is completely free with no hidden fees, no subscriptions, and no limits. You can compress videos, compress PDFs, and convert video formats as many times as you like at no cost.

home.faq.items.q: Do my files get uploaded to a server?
home.faq.items.a: No. All processing happens directly in your browser using WebAssembly technology. Your files never leave your device and are never sent to any server, so your data stays completely private.

home.faq.items.q: Do I need to create an account to use these tools?
home.faq.items.a: No account or sign-up is required. Just open the tool you need and start immediately — no registration, no email, no password.

home.faq.items.q: What tools are available on FileYuzu?
home.faq.items.a: FileYuzu currently offers a free online video compressor, a PDF compressor, and a video converter supporting formats like MP4, WebM, MOV, MKV, and MP3. More tools are being added regularly.

home.faq.items.q: Is there a file size limit?
home.faq.items.a: There is no hard server-side limit since all processing happens in your browser. In practice, most tools handle files up to 500 MB. Performance depends on your device's memory and processing power.

home.faq.items.q: Can I use FileYuzu on my phone or tablet?
home.faq.items.a: Yes! FileYuzu is fully responsive and works on smartphones, tablets, and desktop computers. No app installation is needed — just open the site in your mobile browser and use any tool.

compress_video.faq.title: Video Compressor FAQ

compress_video.faq.items.q: Is my video uploaded to a server?
compress_video.faq.items.a: No. All compression is done locally using FFmpeg.wasm (WebAssembly). Your video file never leaves your device.
We support MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, WebM, and most other common formats. Output is always MP4 for maximum device compatibility.

compress_video.faq.items.q: What is the maximum file size?
compress_video.faq.items.a: The video can be up to 500 MB. For larger files, consider splitting the video first or using a desktop tool like HandBrake.

compress_video.faq.items.q: What do the Low, Balance, and High presets mean?
compress_video.faq.items.a: Balance reduces size by around 60–80% while keeping the video looking good on most screens. Low produces the smallest file with more visible quality loss. High keeps near-original quality with moderate reduction.
It strips the audio track from the compressed video. Useful for mute clips, screen recordings, or when you need the smallest possible file size.

compress_video.faq.items.q: Why do the platform tags show specific MB values?
compress_video.faq.items.a: Each platform has an attachment size limit. Gmail is 25 MB, Discord 8 MB (free), Slack 100 MB, and so on. Clicking a tag sets the target size automatically.

compress_video.faq.items.q: Will there be a visible quality drop?
compress_video.faq.items.a: It depends on the preset and original video. For most videos, the Balance preset produces output that looks fine on phones and standard screens. You can try a different preset if you are not satisfied.
